Football.title: Ukraine national team ‘robbed in Benidorm as thieves steal thousands of euros worth of watches and luxury items’
Football.content:
UKRAINE’S squad was rocked ahead of their World Cup play-off showdown with Sweden – after they were ROBBED during their training camp.

Arsenal striker Viktor Gyokeres notched a hat-trick as former West Ham and Chelsea chief Graham Potter guided his side to within touching distance of the summer extravaganza.





But members of Ukraine’s national team, bossed by ex-Tottenham striker Sergei Rebrov, could be forgiven if their mind was elsewhere when they suffered a 3-1 loss.

Record report that thieves broke into their hotel when they were in Benidorm preparing for the showdown.

Ukraine’s biggest names were stunned to find that thousands of euros worth of watches and luxury items, along with cash, had been stolen.

It resulted in the squad flying to Valencia, where the clash with Sweden took place, without cherished belongings.



Ukrainian journalist Igor Burbas said: “Before the trip to Valencia, the national team was literally robbed.

“It happened at the hotel, when they were out of their rooms.

“Unknown individuals broke into the rooms and robbed the players and coaching staff.

“They stole money, luxury items and watches valued at thousands of euros.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET UP TO £100 BONUS

“Alberto Bosch, Sergei Rebrov’s assistant, had €1,800 stolen from him, for example. What kind of state of mind can the team have after that?”

Everton defender Vitaliy Mykolenko and Brentford midfielder Yehor Yarmolyuk were among the names in Ukraine’s starting line-up against Sweden.

But Gyokeres put them to the sword by notching a sensational hat-trick at the home of LaLiga side Levante.



Matviy Ponomarenko grabbed what proved to only be a consolation in the closing stages.

Sweden will now face Poland on March 31 with a last-gasp ticket to the World Cup up for grabs.

Football.title: Roy Hodgson is addicted to football at 78 and could make job PERMANENT after shock reunion at EFL club 44 years on
Football.content:
YOU’D have thought that after half a century in management, Roy Hodgson would have had enough.

Enough of the criticism and moans, enough of the nightmare of dealing with players – especially given that many of them are young enough to be his great-grandson.





But that ignores the reality that football is a drug.

And one that is very hard to wean yourself off.

Hodgson’s shock return to the Bristol City bench, 44 years after he was first appointed by the Ashton Gate club and at the age of 78, is further evidence that the dressing room buzz remains an irresistible draw.

Perhaps, though, it was inevitable that the oldest manager to take charge of a Premier League game would also want to set an EFL record as well.

Hodgson’s greatest strength has always been that sheer enthusiasm for the game, one taught by his bus driver dad Bill as he grew up in Croydon amid the privations of post-World War Two food rationing.

The former schoolteacher who went on to take England to the 2014 World Cup – admittedly, not the Three Lions’ finest hour on the global stage – refused to accept it was over after the Iceland nightmare at Euro 2016.

For Hodgson, management was a test of his mettle and endurance – but one in which his experience enabled a degree of perspective.

Part-way through the first of his two post-England spells at Crystal Palace, he explained: “It is a sadistic pleasure. The suffering never stops -that’s the problem.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET UP TO £100 BONUS

“You learn to harden yourself towards it but, the longer you are in, it isn’t something you can give up lightly.

“It’s not something you can walk away from and, even if you’re not winning, it’s possible to derive some satisfaction from the fact you are working properly.

“But I suffer during games. We follow the action, kicking every ball, wondering if our centre-backs can stop the cross … In some ways you enjoy it but your heart is always thumping.”

That is the adrenaline rush that has propelled Hodgson back into the hotseat, more than two years after he “stepped down” at Selhurst Park, paving the way for Oliver Glasner.

And Hodgson is not the only old stager who cannot exit from the thrill of the lights.



Even this month, Neil Warnock – a comparative kid at just 77 and who has had more comebacks than Frank Sinatra – agreed a short-term stint as caretaker at Torquay, although that lasted only a couple of weeks.

Bobby Robson and Sir Alex Ferguson both kept at the gig into their 70s, as did Claudio Ranieri and Guus Hiddink, while Uruguay’s former Argentine, Chile and Leeds chief Marcelo Bielsa will be 71 two days after the World Cup Final.

Otto Rehhagel with Greece, Uruguay’s Oscar Tabarez, Dutch legend Louis van Gaal and Italian Cesare Maldini, with Paraguay, were all into their eighth decade at World Cups while Dick Advocaat, 78, steered Curacao to the greatest show on earth this summer only to step down for family reasons.

For now, according to the Robins, Hodgson has replaced the sacked Gerhard Struber as an “interim” and “until the end of the season”.

But without a sporting director in place, Hodgson will be aware that this may not just be a temporary return and move out of his home in south west London.



If he can arrest the slide that cost Struber his role – exactly as he did when he replaced Patrick Vieira with Palace seemingly doomed in 2023 – City might feel that what they need is a steady hand, a man who will not be flustered, rather than gambling on a new face.

There is also a sense of a second chance to make a first impression at the club where his English managerial career began and ended after just four months in 1982.

Recalling his spell in the south west, he said: “Bristol City was nothing short of a disaster in that we had only been there for a matter of weeks before the banks started to pull the rug from underneath the club.

“My job when I eventually took over, as caretaker manager, was quite simply to carry on in the aftermath of all the players leaving the club and just fulfilling the fixtures.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Now Hodgson is back where, in English terms, it all started.



And if he is offered the chance to have one final season at the helm, it’s hard to believe Hodgson would turn it down. Like other addicts, he might just want the next hit.

Football.title: Carlos Baleba transfer update with Man Utd set to take advantage of bargain fee for £100m Brighton star
Football.content:
CARLOS BALEBA will be a man in demand again this summer after seeing a dream move to Manchester United fall through last summer.

The Cameroon midfielder, who told The Sun last year that he wanted to be the best player in the world, is determined to take his game to the next level and wants to play in the Champions League.





A few things have worked both for and against Baleba since United made an enquiry for him last summer, one being the fact that he hasn’t really hit the heights of last season.

He’s been subbed off at half-time on SEVEN occasions this season, four times at club level and three for the national team.

He did show a glimpse of his prowess when he went away for AFCON, but it came more in flashes than with real consistency.

What his inconsistency has done, though, is lead Brighton to lower the £100 million valuation they had for him last summer.

With United still lurking, this is music to the ears of the midfielder’s entourage.

Despite what has clearly been a challenging season, Baleba has still managed 19 league starts for Brighton and continues to be a regular in Fabian Hurzeler’s side.

Last season, his English was, at best, average, but he’s spent the last few months improving it.

He also flew his family in from Cameroon to stay with him as he navigates what has been a difficult campaign and tries to rediscover his best form for the remainder of the season.



BEST FREE BETS AND BETTING SIGN UP OFFERS

Well-placed sources at Brighton have also said that, unlike last season, when the club were totally against a sale and unwilling to shake up their midfield, their stance has softened.

The club have bought themselves time since making it clear they were not going to sell, and have scouted Ghanaian international Caleb Yirenkyi, who plays for Nordsjaelland and is set to feature for Ghana at the World Cup this summer.

Yirenkyi is from the same academy that produced the likes of Mohammed Kudus, and SunSport has been reliably told that Brighton, Arsenal and Bournemouth have all sent scouts to Denmark to take a closer look at the midfielder.



Sources within Brighton view this as the ideal time to let go of Baleba, who joined the club from Lille in 2023 as a replacement for Moises Caicedo.

For a club like Brighton and the way they are run, three seasons is enough to develop a player, move him on, make a profit, and reinvest it in another budding talent, a model that has proven successful over the years.

Football.title: Heartache for Irish football icon Roy Keane as ‘beloved mother’ Marie dies ‘surrounded by loving family’ & tributes paid
Football.content:
LEGEND MOURNS Heartache for Irish football icon Roy Keane as ‘beloved mother’ Marie dies ‘surrounded by loving family’ & tributes paid

IRELAND legend Roy Keane is mourning the sad death of his mother Marie whom has passed away “surrounded by her loving family”.

Her rip.ie post explains that she died peacefully at Marymount University Hospital and Hospice.





The post reads: “Keane (Carhoo, Rathpeacon, Co. Cork); on March 27th 2026, peacefully, surrounded by her loving family and in the tender loving care of the doctors, nurses and staff of St. Anne’s Ward at Marymount University Hospital and Hospice; Marie (née Lynch); dearly loved wife of the late Maurice (Mossie) and beloved mother of Denis, Johnson, Hilary, Roy and Pat.

“Predeceased by her brother Pat, her niece Estella and her nephew Thomas.



“Sadly missed by her loving family, sisters Ina, Annette and Josephine, brothers Jack, Leo, Michael and Noel, grandchildren, great-grandchildren, daughters-in-law Linda, Theresa and Mary, brothers-in-law, sisters-in-law, nephews, nieces, relatives, her great neighbours and friends.”

It then states that her funeral mass is to take place on Tuesday March 31 at 11am. It will be held at the Church of the Resurrection, Farranree.

She is to be laid to rest at St. Catherine’s Cemetery, Kilcully.

The family have asked that in lieu of flowers, donations be made to Marymount University Hospice in her memory.



Roy, 54, has always been a private person who’s tended to shy away from sharing too much about his family life.

But when he did speak of Marie, it was obvious how deep his love was for her. He once affectionately referred to her as “the only boss I listen to”.

And last year, he memorably choked up while showing his punditry pals on Stick to Football old photos from early in his career when his parents had come to see him play at the old Wembley Stadium.

He went into reflective mood while passing the photos around the group. Roy noted: “You’re on about photos, look at that there.

“You’re on about family, the ups and downs of football. But when you see your family at Wembley after you’ve won a big game…”



Ian Wright, Jill Scott and Gary Neville had to take over the conversation as they noticed Keane was rendered speechless due to being overcome at the sight of his parents looking on proudly at the home of English football.

As noted in the rip.ie post, Roy’s father Maurice or ‘Mossie’ died back in August 2019.

More to follow…

Football.title: ‘I can see the light’ – Tottenham star Dejan Kulusevski reveals return date after missing entire season through injury
Football.content:
DEJAN KULUSEVSKI is convinced he will be fit to play at the World Cup with Sweden.

The Tottenham star, 25, has been out injured all season as a result of kneecap surgery in May last year.



A fortnight ago he went under the knife again but says his knee now “feels great” and believes he will be ready for this summer’s feast of football.



Sweden beat Ukraine on Thursday night to set up a play-off with Poland in Stockholm on Tuesday where the winner will qualify for the finals.

Kulusevski told Swedish media outlet Viaplay: “[The World Cup] is the driving force I have. To have that opportunity is absolutely fantastic.

“It’s a challenge that I haven’t been able to overcome so far in terms of getting healthy, but it’s not over yet. Now the second game starts.”

Kulusevski has not played since injured his patella in a challenge with Marc Guehi during Spurs’ 2-0 home defeat to Crystal Palace last season.



He had hoped to back by Autumn but the injury has proved more complicated than he hoped, meaning he is still some way off action.

But his latest comments suggest he could be back in time to help Spurs with their relegation fight as well as featuring at the World Cup.

He added: “I understand that people were worried, but it was really for the wrong reason.

CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

“It was really positive that I did this little procedure and that we were able to find the problem.



“Now everything should be solved. Now it’s just a matter of slowly and surely coming back.

“I can see the light. It feels better now. I really have a positive feeling about these two important matches (for Sweden) and a World Cup in two or three months.

“This is how the book and the chapter will end. I am convinced of that.

“That’s how it has to end. Otherwise, it hasn’t gone as I planned.

“I’m absolutely convinced that we’ll win these two games and I’ll be ready to play and play my first World Cup in the USA. That’s how the movie will end, God willing.”

Football.title: England star’s girlfriend stuns in bold sleeveless outfit as fans label her ‘unreal’
Football.content:
CHELSEA superstar Cole Palmer’s girlfriend Olivia Holder took social media by storm during a recent holiday.

Palmer, 23, is currently on England duty ahead of tonight’s international friendly with Uruguay at Wembley.





The playmaker will also be available for Tuesday’s clash with Japan in London.

Olivia, 23, appears to have gone on holiday in Bucharest recently where she tried on an outfit that did the rounds.

The aesthetic clinic co-owner posed at a Romanian bar and shared snaps for her 20,400 Instagram followers that stormed the comments’ section.

One fan posted: “Unreal.”

Another commented: “Wow wow wow.”



A third wrote: “The IT girl.”

This fan said: “Big slay.”

And that one added: “Omg.”

Palmer looks set to get on the plane for England‘s World Cup campaign this summer in the US, Canada and Mexico.

The Chelsea star has amassed a total of two goals in all of his 12 caps since his Three Lions debut in 2023.



This comes amid another impressive campaign with the Blues that has seen him register 10 goals and three assists in 25 appearances across all competitions.

Palmer and Olivia’s blossoming romance kicked off late last year with the pair seen making a trip to Winter Wonderland.

She has since been to a number of Chelsea games supporting Palmer, including matches at Stamford Bridge against Brentford and Leeds.

Football.title: World Cup’s sexiest fan Ivana Knoll stuns in bold see-through outfit as she gets ready for opener against England
Football.content:
THE World Cup’s sexiest fan stunned in a bold see-through outfit.

Ivana Knoll made headlines in Qatar with her stunning and daring looks.





The Croatia superfan backed her team with an array of dazzling displays featuring her team colours.

Now an influencer with 2.8million followers, Ivana has ditched the colour for an all-black style.

Ivana posed seductively in the see-through jumpsuit in a series of photos she shared online.

Her fans flocked to the post to shower her with compliments.



One said: “You are a beautiful charm 😍”

Another added: “You’re so beautiful Ivana!😍”

A third wrote: “So elegant and sexy ❤️”

And another commented: “Absolutely gorgeous ❤️”



Ivana, 33, has recently taken up a new career as a DJ and has enjoyed immediate success.

She has performed across Europe, the Middle East and in North America under her stage name KnollDoll.

And she played her biggest show to date after being announced as a performer at this summer’s World Cup.

Ivana performed DJ set during half-time of the match between her beloved Croatia and Colombia on Thursday.

Football.title: Man Utd loanee Jadon Sancho ‘leaves pitch in a sling’ after injury blow as Aston Villa fans fume at ‘pointless friendly’
Football.content:
JADON SANCHO suffered another setback after being forced off during a friendly between Aston Villa and Elche on Friday.

The 26-year-old left the pitch in the 39th minute after sustaining what appeared to be a shoulder injury.



Sancho looked very uncomfortable as he walked across the pitch with his right arm in a makeshift sling.



Fans were left concerned, with many also furious that Villa had even organised a friendly during the international break.

One person wrote: “What is the point in this stupid friendly.”

Another commented: “Sack whoever thought a friendly during the run in was a good idea.”

A third said: “Hopefully it’s precautionary and it’s just bruised.”

While a fourth added: “We have to be the unluckiest club in the world.”

The incident happened with Villa 1-0 up in the first half.

Pau Torres broke the deadlock for the Premier League side, but Federico Redondo equalised on the brink of half-time.

CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

Alvaro Rodriguez then scored the winner for Elche in the final minute.



Villa announced earlier this week that they were flying to Spain for a three-day training camp.

Sancho has made 31 appearances for the club this term, scoring once and providing three assists.

He joined on loan from Manchester United in September.

Football.title: USA on course to PLAY Iran in World Cup clash as FIFA face nightmare situation
Football.content:
USA on course to PLAY Iran in World Cup clash as FIFA face nightmare situation

The United States could potentially face Iran at this summer's World Cup as a prediction emerges

View 2 Images FIFA could face a nightmare situation at this summer's World Cup in the USA (Image: AP)

The United States have been projected to potentially face Iran at this summer’s World Cup amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The US will co-host this summer’s tournament alongside Canada and Mexico, which will take centre stage across June and July.

‌



England are also heading across the Atlantic this summer for the World Cup, with manager Thomas Tuchel helping oversee a perfect qualification campaign with a 100 percent record.

‌



Former Chelsea and Tottenham Hotspur boss Mauricio Pochettino is currently the manager of the United States Men’s National Team (USMNT), who will compete in Group D in the tournament.

‌



The US will face Paraguay, Australia and either Turkey or Kosovo in the group stages of the World Cup, with the last remaining team to be decided via the play-offs during this international break.

Iran footballers take stand over US bombing of school in match ahead of World Cup READ MORE :

FIFA suddenly scrap 2,000 World Cup hotel bookings in latest bizarre twist around tournament READ MORE :

Turkey are the favourites to get past Kosovo and join the United States in Group D of this summer’s World Cup and as a result, a prediction has been made for the potential fixtures and path to the final.

A report in The Sun, which has compiled data from Football Meets Data, predicts that Turkey will emerge from the group as winners while the United States will finish as runner-up.

‌



In Group G, Iran are joined by Belgium, New Zealand and Egypt. If Iran was to finish runner-up in the group, and the Us finish second in Group D, then both countries would go on to meet in the next round.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

AT&T Stadium in Dallas, Texas, will host the round of 32 match on July 3 that could see the United States face Iran in the first knockout fixture of the World Cup.

‌



Iran’s participation at this summer’s World Cup has been a topic of uncertainty ever since the conflict in the Middle East began last month.

US President Donald Trump confirmed on February 28 that a military operation was carried out in Iran. This led to Iran launching retaliatory strikes, and the country’s place in the World Cup this summer has been cast into doubt as a result.

View 2 Images Mauricio Pochettino will take charge of the US at this summer's World Cup (Image: (Photo by Julio Aguilar/Getty Images))

‌



Andrew Giuliani, head of the White House’s World Cup task force, recently stated that he expects Iran to compete at this summer’s World Cup despite the growing uncertainty.

"Right now, we're expecting the Iranian team to come,” Giuliani said, via the BBC. “I think it's a great opportunity for the Iranian diaspora here in the United States of America to be able to celebrate their country.

"We're doing everything we can to make sure it's safe and secure, but we need to open up the Department of Homeland Security to maximise our chances that this is not just a safe and secure World Cup, but truly an incredible success.”

Football.title: Roy Keane's mother Marie dies as family issue statement
Football.content:
Roy Keane's mother, Marie, has sadly died. It was confirmed on Friday that she had 'passed away peacefully' while 'surrounded by loved ones' at Marymount Hospice.

A notice on RIP.ie confirmed the news, saying she will be "sadly missed by her loving family, sisters Ina, Annette and Josephine, brothers Jack, Leo, Michael and Noel, grandchildren, great-grandchildren, daughters-in-law Linda, Theresa and Mary, brothers-in-law, sisters-in-law, nephews, nieces, relatives, her great neighbours and friends."

Roy was known to be close to his mother and often travelled home to see her. He shared a photograph of the two of them on one such visit, quipping she was 'the only boss he'd listen to.'

The Manchester United legend became emotional during an episode of the Stick to Football podcast last year when he came across a picture of his parents while going through mementos from his illustrious playing career.

Rockmount AFC, the schoolboy club Keane played for as a youngster in Cork, were among the first to offer their condolences.

They said: "Sincere condolences to extended Keane and Lynch family on the passing of Marie."

Football.title: Federico Chiesa's latest blow and Liverpool future after Arne Slot admission on unhappiness
Football.content:
Federico Chiesa's latest blow and Liverpool future after Arne Slot admission on unhappiness

Federico Chiesa has mostly been used as a substitute during his two seasons at the club under Arne Slot

View 2 Images Federico Chiesa has had a tough time since joining Liverpool (Image: Andrew Powell, Liverpool FC via Getty Images)

Liverpool star Federico Chiesa suffered another setback during the latest international break. The Italy international was selected by boss Gennaro Gattuso for the Azzurri's World Cup qualifying play-offs despite his limited game time at club level, but was forced to withdraw from the squad.

‌



Chiesa has endured a difficult period since his move to Liverpool from Juventus in 2024, and this represented yet another disappointment. Speculation continues to surround the Euro 2020 winner's club future, though fans may need to wait to see what's next for him.

‌



"Not everyone is the same," Gattuso said when discussing Chiesa. "Footballers' minds aren't all alike. When I hear he has problems, that he's hesitant, I have to make a decision.

‌



"We decided together because he didn't feel up to it; he went back home, and I accepted it. I can try to convince him for a while, but I always think that in life, beyond a match, relationships matter.

"I don't have great medical expertise, but if someone isn't feeling well, you shouldn't insist. If he ends up damaging his knee, how is that player supposed to look at me? So I don't push it - I make him understand. When someone feels they're not at 100%, it's right that they go home."

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Jurgen Klopp admitted Mohamed Salah gesture nearly made him cry after 'selfish' claim READ MORE :

Bayern Munich star thinks he knows Michael Olise's future plans as Liverpool circle READ MORE :

Chiesa was one of only two signings made by Arne Slot during his first Liverpool transfer window and the sole arrival to join straight away, with Giorgi Mamardashvili not due at Anfield until 2025. The ex-Juventus winger became a Premier League champion at the first time of asking but saw limited action on the pitch.

The 28-year-old barely featured in the league during his debut Reds campaign, making just six appearances as fitness issues derailed his campaign. He's already surpassed that tally this term, though the bulk of his appearances in all competitions - including 22 of his 23 league run-outs - have been as a substitute and some questions have followed.

Champions League frustration

Slot originally omitted Chiesa from his squad for this season's Champions League, before the Italian was handed a reprieve when defender Giovanni Leoni sustained a long-term injury. "Of course, that is one of the most difficult things you have to do as a manager," Slot said in September when discussing his conversations with the forward around the initial decision.

‌



View 2 Images Arne Slot has spoken about his conversations with Chiesa (Image: Justin Setterfield, Getty Images)

"For a player not to play, it is not always easy to tell them but not to be in the squad is always more difficult. I just explained to him why we made this choice. Of course, he wasn't happy with that.

"I think he understood my arguments - that is not to say he agreed with them. He didn't tell me, by the way, that he didn't agree with them! But he gave me the answer you want to hear from a player, that he will be there to help the team in the cups and in the league.

‌



"That's one thing to say but I think he also showed this because he wasn't selected but he worked really hard these days to be ready for the upcoming days. That is even more important than what a player tells you."

What's next for Chiesa

Chiesa remains a cult figure amongst Liverpool supporters, despite not entirely justifying his £12.5million price tag, though the fee could yet prove a bargain, and he continues to feature in Italy's plans. While his chances have been scarce, he has made a strong impression on the occasions he has been called upon and - to his credit - hasn't sought an escape route despite reported interest from Serie A.

"I play for a top club. This is maybe one of the top three in the world. It's Liverpool – of course they are going to buy top players; of course, the competition is high," he said after setting up both goals in a Carabao Cup win against Southampton in September.

‌



England's 2026 World Cup kits This article contains affiliate links, we will receive a commission on any sales we generate from it. Learn more Various Prices England Official Store Buy Now on England Official Store Product Description England and Nike have launched the new home, away and goalkeeper kits to be worn at this summer's FIFA World Cup. You can get free delivery on all orders with the code: ENGFREEDEL

"If I don't want competition, I go to another team. But I want the competition, I want to play here and I think, at the end of the day, competition helps you to become a better player."

Chiesa put pen to paper on a four-year contract upon joining Liverpool, meaning the club are under no pressure to sell. Even if they were to entertain the notion of offloading the forward, they needn't fret about losing out on a hefty fee by allowing his contract to wind down, simply because he was never likely to fetch a substantial sum.

Article continues below

With Mohamed Salah set to depart Anfield at the end of the season, further changes to the forward line could well be on the cards. Chiesa's circumstances remain somewhat unusual, though clearer indications may surface once the dust settles on the current campaign.

Football.title: Michael Carrick only has one rival left for Man Utd job as world-class boss drops out
Football.content:
Michael Carrick only has one rival left for Man Utd job as world-class boss drops out

Michael Carrick could soon be in a straight shoot-out with a world class manager for the permanent Manchester United head coach role

View 2 Images Michael Carrick is a candidate for the permanent Manchester United job (Image: (Photo by Charlotte Wilson/Offside/Offside via Getty Images))

Manchester United's search for a new manager should now be down to just two candidates - Michael Carrick and Diego Simeone. The former has absolutely earned the right to be a contender for the permanent role, while Simeone is the most likely elite-level manager to leave his current job for Old Trafford.

‌



For a staggering 15 years, Simeone has been in charge of Atletico Madrid and though he has only been able to deliver four domestic trophies in that time, due to Barcelona and Real Madrid dominance, he has proven himself to be an elite tactician.

‌



It is also worth pointing out that over that period he has won two Europa Leagues and regularly has Atletico in the latter stages of the Champions League.

‌



Qualification for that competition next season is Carrick's main aim with United in a great place to finish in the top five as they sit seven points clear of sixth-placed Chelsea. Club chiefs will have a big decision to make if he achieves that goal with ease.

Marcus Rashford told which Man Utd spot is waiting for him after Barcelona transfer twist READ MORE :

Luis Enrique agreement nears in major development in Man Utd's manager search READ MORE :

The lack of clearly available options to come in permanently only furthers the possibility that he is likely to get the job with Paris Saint-Germain boss Luis Enrique, who was previously mooted as a candidate, not believed to be interested.

Oliver Glasner will join Roberto De Zerbi and Gareth Southgate in being available to United at the end of the season but none of the trio are too enticing given recent managerial spells. Mauricio Pochettino, Carlo Ancelotti and Julian Nagelsmann are other top coaches that could be available following the World Cup.

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

The former, however, is more likely to join Tottenham Hotspur while Ancelotti and Nagelsmann may remain at Brazil and Germany, respectively, if successful in the United States this summer.

That could quite literally leave just Carrick and Simeone as permanent appointments for Sir Jim Ratcliffe and Co. for the 2026/27 season and beyond. Simeone has left the door open to joining United and the 55-year-old will surely be open to a new challenge soon

‌



Both options have been endorsed over the last few months with club legend Roy Keane making his feelings clear on Simeone. Back in October, he said on the Stick to Football podcast: "I've said it for years, I'd like Atletico Madrid's Simeone to go in there. I'd like to see him go there.

View 2 Images Diego Simeone has been linked with the Manchester United job (Image: Getty Images)

"I know his mate has gone to Arsenal [Andrea Berta, who left Atletico to join the Gunners as sporting director]. I think he would just create havoc, but good havoc. He'd rock up to that place. No guarantees, but I'd just like to see his personality, his track record.

‌



"People might think styles of football, [but] they scored five against Real Madrid [winning 5-2] last weekend. He doesn't like his teams giving up too many goals or chances, but they can play a bit and fight.

"At Liverpool [where Atletico lost 3-2 in the Champions League], the team aren't as great as few years ago, but they still had fighting spirit. He's on the sideline, he got sent off that night, I'd like a big personality."

Football.title: Iran footballers take stand over US bombing of school in match ahead of World Cup
Football.content:
Iran footballers take stand over US bombing of school in match ahead of World Cup

The Iranian football team held backpacks while singing the national anthem ahead of their friendly against Nigeria after a school was bombed and up to 180 people were killed in the region. In late February, Shajarah Tayyebeh elementary school was bombed with between 175 and 180 people, most of them girls between the ages of seven and 12, killed.

The tragedy took place near the start of the Middle Eastern conflict, with the Guardian reporting it was one of 6,000 locations struck by the US, having been on a target list as it was considered a military facility. That was even though the school appeared in Iranian business listings and was visible on Google Maps.

Further investigations from a preliminary inquiry, via The New York Times, showed that it was adjacent to an Iranian base, of which the school building was once part of, and that it was a mistake from the US due to the usage of old data provided by the Defense Intelligence Agency.

The US response to the school bombing changed in the aftermath, with President Donald Trump initially unsure about what happened before blaming it on Iran.

He said: "Based on what I’ve seen, that was done by Iran. We think it was done by Iran – because they are very inaccurate, as you know, with their munitions. They have no accuracy whatsoever. It was done by Iran.”

On March 27, comment from the UK government regarding the tragedy read: "We are appalled by all reports of civilian deaths – in Minab in Iran, and across the region.

"Innocent civilians should never be caught in the crossfire. Our thoughts are with the victims and their families. We support all action that will help bring a swift end to this conflict.

"Mr President, even in times of conflict we cannot ignore Iran’s own human rights record, which remains dire. Just two months ago, this Council met to condemn the brutal killing of thousands of Iranian protestors exercising their right to peaceful protest.

Image: Getty Images) Getty Images)

"Iran’s abhorrent use of the death penalty, and continued internet restrictions, fuel cycles of repression for the people of Iran.

"The UK will continue to work with international partners, including the UN Fact Finding Mission and the Special Rapporteur, to hold Iran to account for its repression of women and girls, religious and ethnic minorities, and human rights defenders.

"We know it takes huge bravery to protest and to speak out in the face of this oppression, especially for women and girls who continue to endure severe repression in their daily lives.

"Mr President, the UK demands that the Iranian authorities fully respect human rights and fundamental freedoms of all people in Iran. Thank you."

There is currently uncertainty over Iran's involvement in this summer's World Cup due to the ongoing war. The nation saw a request to play their matches in Mexico turned down by FIFA and it has now emerged that they could even face the US at the last-32 stage.

Simulations from Football Meets Data has determined through likely results that each team will finish as runners-up in their respective groups and thus meet in the first knockout round at AT&T Stadium in Dallas, Texas.

Football.title: Francisco Conceicao speaks out on £52m Liverpool links as Mo Salah successor hunt begins
Football.content:
Francisco Conceicao speaks out on £52m Liverpool links as Mo Salah successor hunt begins

The £52million-rated Juventus winger has addressed the speculation linking him with a move to Liverpool as they search for a Mohamed Salah replacement

View 2 Images Francisco Conceicao has been linked with a transfer to Liverpool (Image: Image Photo Agency/Getty Images)

Juventus star Francisco Conceicao has responded to speculation linking him with Liverpool as the Premier League champions evaluate their transfer possibilities to replace the outgoing Mohamed Salah. Salah stunned fans on Tuesday night (March 24) when he announced his departure from the club at the end of the season after nine trophy-laden years.

‌



Following heartfelt tributes from team-mates, both current and former as well as fans and pundits, focus has begun shifting towards potential targets Liverpool might pursue to replace a player who has netted 255 goals in 435 appearances at Anfield.

‌



One name reportedly under consideration is £52m-rated Juventus and Portugal winger Francisco Conceicao.

‌



The rumours have evidently reached the player after he was questioned about the links prior to Portugal's friendlies with Mexico and the USA during the international break.

Jurgen Klopp admitted Mohamed Salah gesture nearly made him cry after 'selfish' claim READ MORE :

Bayern Munich star thinks he knows Michael Olise's future plans as Liverpool circle READ MORE :

Conceicao said: “I know people talk about a big club, but I play for a big club where I am happy.

“At the moment I am only focused on these two games with the national team and then I will focus on the final stretch of the season to help my club as much as possible.”

‌



Conceicao moved to Juventus, originally on loan, from Porto in August 2024, and after inheriting the No.7 shirt vacated by Federico Chiesa's transfer to Liverpool, he bagged seven goals in 40 appearances last summer.

The 23-year-old was a prime transfer target for the Reds' Merseyside neighbours Everton last summer, but he opted for a €32m (£27.7m) permanent switch to the Serie A giants instead, where he's bagged another four goals in 34 outings this campaign.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



With Juventus locked in a fierce battle to secure Champions League qualification, Conceicao added: “I am at a big club where the pressure to win is very high. I just have to keep working and contribute with goals and assists.

“My objective is to help with goals and assists, that is what I try to do. I must continue every day to show that I have the ability to help the national team and be helped by them to keep improving”.

Conceicao isn't the only winger reportedly on the Reds' shortlist of potential targets.

‌



View 2 Images Francisco Conceicao has addressed rumours about his future amid links with Liverpool (Image: Medios y Media/Getty Images)

Bayern Munich's Michael Olise, Aston Villa's Morgan Rogers, Paris Saint-Germain's Bradley Barcola and RB Leipzig's Yan Diomande have also been suggested as potential successors to Salah.

While Juventus' position on parting with Conceicao remains unclear, Bayern have made it abundantly clear they have zero intention of losing Olise, the ex-Crystal Palace winger who is experiencing the campaign of his career.

‌



Munich chief executive Uli Hoeness said: "Remember Liverpool spent €500m last summer and is having a very bad season.

"So we won't be contributing to them playing better next year."

Meanwhile, Bayern director of sport, Max Eberl, stated: "We're not even wasting a thought on that.

Article continues below

"He is a Bayern Munich player and has every opportunity here that top players could wish for. We want to shape the future with him."

Football.title: FIFA suddenly scrap 2,000 World Cup hotel bookings in latest bizarre twist around tournament
Football.content:
FIFA suddenly scrap 2,000 World Cup hotel bookings in latest bizarre twist around tournament

Preparations are well underway for the 2026 World Cup but there have been a number of logistical problems faced by FIFA, with the latest seeing a mass cancellation of hotel rooms

View 2 Images The FIFA World Cup gets underway in under three months' time (Image: Robbie Jay Barratt - AMA/Getty Images)

FIFA has cancelled over 2,000 hotels rooms in World Cup host Cities just a few months before the start of the tournament. The 2026 edition of the competition will take place in North America with USA, Canada and Mexico jointly hosting.

‌



Preparations are now well underway as the competition draws nearer. Nations are competing over the international break to earn the final slots in the group stages.

‌



There have been a few logistical hurdles for FIFA to overcome, as expected with an event of this scale. The latest has seen a mass cancellation of hotel rooms, particularly in Philadelphia, which will host Ivory Coast vs Ecuador as its first game on June 14.

‌



2,000 of the 10,000 hotel rooms originally booked by FIFA are said to have been cancelled. Speaking to ABC 6, President of the Philadelphia Hotel Association, Ed Grose, said: “While we were not excited about that, it’s not the end of the world either.

Marcus Rashford told which Man Utd spot is waiting for him after Barcelona transfer twist READ MORE :

World Cup insider gives update on Iran boycott after FIFA deny request and Donald Trump post READ MORE :

“These are rooms that are going to be put back out on the marketplace and sold to fans who want to come to Philadelphia.” It’s also been confirmed that a similar thing has happened in Mexico City, with 800 of the 2,000 hotel rooms booked reportedly being cancelled.

An an interview with ESPN, director general of Mexico City’s Hotel Association, Alberto Albarran Leyva said: “FIFA booked 2,000 rooms months ago to prevent [running short] and guarantee its operation. Over time, it cancelled some reservations because it realised that it was no longer going to use them. There is no other reason or other type of context.”

‌



It’s reported that rooms have been cancelled in all of the 16 host cities. A FIFA spokesperson has confirmed that it’s typical practice to overbook rooms for technical staff, teams, referees and operations personnel.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Rooms not needed are then cancelled once the numbers are confirmed closer to the tournament. The move is the latest in a series of decisions made ahead of this summer's tournament.

‌



FIFA have had to break their own rules around stadium branding, with Atlanta’s Mercedes-Benz Stadium unable to cover the branding. The governing body are also pressing American President Donald Trump to relax rules that could require players from certain countries to pay a bond of up to $15,000 (£11,200) to enter the country, with the legislation set to come into affect in April.

Additionally, there has been a huge amount of talk about ticket prices for the tournament. Football Supporters Europe has lodged a complaint with the European Commission over dynamic ticket pricing, which results in higher prices when demand is high.

View 2 Images FIFA are facing many logistical issues over the World Cup (Image: Mike Egerton/PA Wire)

‌



FIFA has responded to the reported complaint, saying they are ensuring ‘fair access to our game for existing and prospective fans.’ They also point to the World Cup's profits being invested in the growth of the game.

There have also been concerns about security preparations for the World Cup, with nominee for the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), Markwayne Mullin, admitting they are ‘behind’. The war between the USA and Iran has also raised doubts over the latter's involvement in the tournament.

The World Cup kicks off on 11 June, with Mexico taking on South Africa in Group A at 8pm. England start their campaign on 17 June against Croatia (9pm kick-off), before facing Ghana (23 June, 9pm) and Panama (27 June, 10pm).

Football.title: Jamie Carragher lashes out at Liverpool critics after Mo Salah statement goes down badly
Football.content:
Jamie Carragher lashes out at Liverpool critics after Mo Salah statement goes down badly

After Mohamed Salah announced he was leaving Liverpool, Jamie Carragher has caught some flak for a collaboration he did with the club on social media

View 2 Images Jamie Carragher has defended himself and Liverpool (Image: YouTube/The Overlap)

Jamie Carragher has responded to Liverpool supporters who accused him of showing disrespect toward Mohamed Salah, following his placement of the Egyptian at No. 6 in his ranking of the club's all-time greats. On Tuesday, Salah announced he would be departing the club when the season concludes.

‌



He confirmed his decision by sharing an emotional video showcasing numerous highlights from his time at the club. Two days later, club media published an interview they had conducted with Carragher, in which he ranked who, in his opinion, are the finest ever players to play for the team.

‌



On the former Liverpool defender's ranking, Salah appeared as sixth, behind Ian Rush, John Barnes, Graeme Souness, Sir Kenny Dalglish and Steven Gerrard.

‌



England's 2026 World Cup kits This article contains affiliate links, we will receive a commission on any sales we generate from it. Learn more Various Prices England Official Store Buy Now on England Official Store Product Description England and Nike have launched the new home, away and goalkeeper kits to be worn at this summer's FIFA World Cup. You can get free delivery on all orders with the code: ENGFREEDEL

Numerous social media users criticised Carragher for showing disrespect to Salah regarding the timing of its publication, barely 48 hours following the Egyptian's confirmed exit, reports the Liverpool Echo.

In his defence, Carragher maintained there was no disrespect meant while also clarifying it was the club's internal media department who released the video. He tweeted: "It's not poor timing from anyone, I did this interview before Salah announced his decision.

‌



FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

"Then [I] went on the overlap a day after the Salah announcement and brought up I'd put him 6th on the LFC list. The club decided to put it out before I gave away my full list!"

After another social media user implied Carragher was attempting to have a dig at Salah, considering the war of words they've engaged in throughout this campaign, the former centre-back responded forcefully once more.

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

He wrote: "I'm not one of his biggest critics, when have I ever criticised Salah the player. I said he's the 2nd best foreign player ever in the PL!

"You and plenty of others don't know enough about the club's history and are being very disrespectful to other players on that list!"

‌



View 2 Images Mohamed Salah announced he is leaving Liverpool at the end of the campaign (Image: MI News/NurPhoto)

On The Overlap, discussing Salah's departure, Carragher suggested Liverpool's all-time leading Premier League scorer might consider returning to Italy, where he featured for Fiorentina and AS Roma prior to his Anfield switch.

Carragher said: "I think Mo Salah, the way he's driven like a Ronaldo, I don't see the Saudi thing just yet.

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

"I can see him in Italy with one of the giants. I just think your career's over when you go there [Saudi Arabia]. I think he'll still be looking at his Champions League record or how many goals he can get.

"I still think he thinks, 'I'm still one of the best players, and me leaving now is not the end of my top-level career, I've still got something to give'."

Football.title: The Flat season is back! Who wins the Lincoln?
Football.content:
The Flat season is back and kicks off with the typically competitive William Hill Lincoln Handicap on Doncaster's Town Moor. Sky Sports Racing's senior form analyst has taken a deep dive through this year's field and makes his selection for the big race.

The Race

Fundamentally it's a high-value mile handicap, but figuratively it's so much more than that, the race heightened by history and amplified by appointment as the first page turned of a new season, the Lincoln as much a symbol as a horserace, hence its pulling power year on year.

For the first of William Haggas' wins, in 1992, High Low did it off a mark of 75, which wouldn't get you into the Spring Mile nowadays, and the coating of class in the modern-day Lincoln is highlighted by half a dozen horses rated 100+ this renewal.

The Trends

The trends tend to be temporary or transitional, meaning they're not really trends at all. It's less trends and more waves, the last four winners all aged five or older and high mileage but high compatibility with big fields on a straight mile, in contrast to the prior five on the roll of honour who were all lightly-raced four-year-olds, four of them based at Newmarket.

The last two winners were rated in the low 90s, but eight of the nine before that had a three-figure mark, though only two in the last 15 years were the proverbial Group horse in a handicap, namely Penitent and Addeybb.

Two of the last four winners had contested the Balmoral on Champions Day at the end of their previous season, but as many as seven fulfil that criteria this time, spearheaded by SHOUT who did the best of them that day in fourth, though TRIBAL CHIEF caught the eye in seventh after a luckless run.

The Trainers

The aforementioned Shout and Tribal Chief are from stables who have targeted the Lincoln recently, the Crisfords having two near-misses with Saleymm and Awaal, the latter finishing second to David Menuisier's Migration.

Seven other Lincoln-winning yards have representatives in this edition, but there's one that stands above all, William Haggas the top trainer in the race's long history with five wins, and ETERNAL FORCE has all the ingredients to make it six.

A low-mileage four-year-old from that stable whose improving steps became leaps as last season went on, Eternal Force is a profiler's dream, but naturally there's a premium on his price, and he's not the only horse of that advantageous age.

The Four-Year-Olds

It would take something unusual to stop the brightly-lit Eternal Force from going off favourite, but LA BOTTE is indeed something unusual.

There's catching the eye and then there's what he did under Spencer in the Trial at Wolverhampton, with the best run-out speed by far on the day, and he also topped the same metric in the 30-runner Britannia at Royal Ascot where he beat all bar one, looking every inch a pattern performer of the future. La Botte is an anagram of 'a lot bet', which there will be on him, the stand-out contender.

Charlie Appleby won the Lincoln with four-year-olds in 2016 (Secret Brief) and 2019 (Auxerre), and there are elements of both in ANNO DOMINI, who's had only a handful of races and coming from a stint in Dubai where neither race got to the bottom of him. He's carrying just 9-1 with Toby Moore's claim, and that's a low weight for one who tackled the Group 1 Futurity (at a single-figure price) in his third race.

ROGUE DIPLOMAT ended last year on a roll, with four handicap wins on the bounce (including two at Doncaster), all by less than a length, but they were all at 7f, and the worry is that going this far first time out may reignite his free-going fire.

Image: Anno Domini will be ridden by Ryan Moore's son Toby

The Regulars

Irish raider ORANDI was third last year, though came into that with the Irish Lincolnshire already in the bag, and he failed to fire in the same race a fortnight ago when 11-2 and ridden by Ryan Moore. Just behind Orandi 12 months ago, in fifth, was GALERON, who's 7lbs lower in the weights now despite ending 2025 on a higher note when runner-up back at Doncaster, where he has run very well on all four visits. For a horse who was once fourth in a Guineas, he's potentially very well handicapped, even more so with Jack Callan's claim.

On this day for the last two years, ALPHA CRUCIS has finished fourth then eighth in the Lincoln, while THUNDER ROAR has finished second in two Spring Miles, and it's odd to see him so high up the Lincoln weights at the age of seven.

The 'regulars' doesn't just pertain to Doncaster, as there are other straight-track specialists, such as GREEK ORDER who was fourth in the Royal Hunt Cup, one place ahead of URBAN LION, who maintained high standards until the very end of the season when he went off 9-1 fifth-favourite for the Cambridgeshire but found himself in the wrong group. He won first time out last season. GREAT ACCLAIM has loads of winning form on straight courses but usually takes a few runs to get up to speed, plus he's reappearing without the blinkers that took him to a new level last summer.

The Wildcards

In real terms he's got top weight but his handicap mark was higher still when BOTANICAL was second in the 2024 John Smith's Cup, a touch of class that none of his rivals can match, and he'll make them all go if the switch to Karl Burke has revived him, more likely than not given the trainer's talent. And don't forget that Burke has won the last two Cambridgshires, both carrying top weight and in these colours.

He's a law unto himself but if VALVANO is ever to reveal his true identity then it will be here, first time out with his adrenaline up, blinkered and tongue tied for the first time. He flatters to deceive and hasn't won since his two-year-old debut, but he's got a race like this in him if the mood takes him, and the more rain the better.

Image: Valvano was fifth in the November Handicap at Doncaster in 2024

Jamie Lynch's Verdict

Even though they haven't registered a win since 2021, the four-year-olds are invariably the first port of call for a Lincoln, and La Botte could be one of the really good ones to come here like Penitent and Addeybb who likewise were having just their sixth start at Doncaster.

But there's no meat in his price, and each-way swing on URBAN LION is my preferred play. He ran really well time and again in races of this nature last year and being drawn next to Botanical could be a sweet spot in a race without any other identifiable formation focal point.

Football.title: What would England do without Kane?
Football.content:
On this day 11 years ago, Harry Kane came on as a substitute for Wayne Rooney at Wembley to make his England debut against Lithuania. Eighty seconds later, he was wheeling off to celebrate scoring his first England goal.

Since then, Kane has gone on to be England's all-time leading scorer with 78 goals, and the single most important player for his country for a decade. Most feel that England cannot win the World Cup without him fit and firing.

But Thomas Tuchel has to prepare for a potential nightmare scenario where Kane isn't available. Injury, suspension, heat-exhaustion - all are possible reasons why Kane could be KO'd.

So what's the plan B up top? Who is Kane's natural understudy? At Wembley against Uruguay on Friday, in the penultimate friendly before the England boss has to pick his World Cup squad, opportunity knocks for the two Dominics.

"It's important the two of them understand it's a big chance to be in camp without Harry," Tuchel said of Solanke and Calvert-Lewin. "To compete for a real chance to start and get good quality minutes.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sky Sports senior reporter Rob Dorsett rounds up Thomas Tuchel's key considerations as England head into their final two matches before he names his squad for the World Cup this summer

"Harry is Harry, our captain. A starter for us. The question is if we are chasing a result do we really take Harry off? When is there a chance to be a sub for Harry? Or do we look for someone who will play with Harry? Or someone who is a good penalty taker? These are the questions we have to answer."

The hard truth is that with Kane turning 33 a fortnight after the World Cup final, and a packed workload for Bayern Munich leading up to the tournament, he will categorically not be able to play 90 minutes for a possible eight matches in the North American heat and humidity.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player England and Manchester City midfielder Phil Foden stuns team-mates with a 180 on the darts board. Video credit: @England

Tuchel has recognised he has to protect his prize asset at all costs - evidenced by the extra five days he has given him to rest this week, along with 10 other high-impact players.

Marcus Rashford, Phil Foden and Jarrod Bowen can all do a job as a No 9 in Kane's absence, but Tuchel has been clear that he wants another natural striker as part of his tournament squad. When I asked him in November whether he might take only Kane to the World Cup as his specialist striker, he answered pretty categorically that he doubted it, because he wouldn't feel his squad had the right 'balance'.

It appears Tuchel has narrowed his options down to three possible players: Ollie Watkins, Solanke and Calvert-Lewin.

Datawrapper Datawrapper , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Datawrapper cookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the This content is provided by, which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enablecookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the Privacy Options Unfortunately we have been unable to verify if you have consented to Datawrapper cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Datawrapper cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

Watkins has been left out of this squad because of his collapse in confidence, which has seen him score only two Premier League goals since January 3. He does though have credit in the bank, having played a key role for England at various times, not least in the Euros semi-final with the late winner against the Netherlands two summers ago.

Solanke has not played for his country since Lee Carsley's spell as caretaker manager. His season has been plagued by injury, but a goalscoring return of six in 17 appearances in all competitions isn't too bad, considering Tottenham's woeful form.

Calvert-Lewin's England exile has been even longer. He hasn't represented his country since the quarter-finals of Euro 2020, when he came off the bench to replace Kane against Ukraine. Ten goals in 27 apps for Leeds this season have showed what he can do when he's fit and has a run of games.

It is significant that Tuchel mentioned penalties when talking of Kane's possible replacement. It is a key consideration, not only within the 90 minutes of World Cup matches, but also for any shoot-outs that may come in the knockout stages.

Datawrapper Datawrapper , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Datawrapper cookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the This content is provided by, which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enablecookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the Privacy Options Unfortunately we have been unable to verify if you have consented to Datawrapper cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Datawrapper cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

It seems pretty clear that Ivan Toney is not in Tuchel's plans right now, and so his next best penalty taker - other than Kane - isn't even a striker. Cole Palmer's penalty success rate is outstanding, and may be an important factor in his battle with the other No 10's at England's disposal.

As for the three back-up strikers currently in Tuchel's eye-line, there is little to choose between them from 12 yards, and in truth, none of them has put their hand up as penalty specialist.

So what of their other attributes? Again, the statistics suggest there is a huge fall-off when compared to captain Kane.

It is clear Tuchel has a problem. Arguably, his biggest problem with the World Cup in mind. Kane represents England's biggest strength, but without him, it looks like England's biggest weakness.

Football.title: Ex-England boss Hodgson, 78, takes over at Bristol City as Struber sacked
Football.content:
Roy Hodgson has made a shock return to management as interim Bristol City boss after the Championship club sacked Gerhard Struber.

The former England and Liverpool boss, 78, whose last job in management ended when he left Crystal Palace in February 2024, takes over until the end of the season.

Hodgson returns to Bristol City for a second stint 44 years after an ill-fated four months as manager in 1982 in the old fourth division. The Robins were the first English club he ever managed.

Twitter Twitter , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Twitter cookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the This content is provided by, which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enablecookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the Privacy Options Unfortunately we have been unable to verify if you have consented to Twitter cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Twitter cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

Bristol City, who are winless in their last six, say Struber was dismissed along with assistant Bernd Eibler because "recent performances have not met expectations".

The club sit 16th in the Championship, nine points adrift of the play-off places with seven games to play.

Hodgson will meet the squad on Monday and take charge of his first game against Charlton on Easter Friday, live on Sky Sports.

"I have had great conversations with the board and I am really excited by the opportunity to help until the end of the season," said Hodgson.

"We will get straight to work and look for a positive performance on Good Friday."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Bristol City were beaten at home by West Brom last weekend

Hodgson has been brought in to "set the standards and the values at the club", according to Bristol City chief executive Charlie Boss, himself only appointed last month, as they search for a new sporting director and next permanent head coach.

"I would like to thank Gerhard and Bernd for their hard work over the past nine months and we wish them all the best," Boss said. "Roy's appointment is about more than the results of the next seven games.

"Roy is a vastly experienced coach who has achieved and won at the highest level. He will support me, our players and our football staff as we build towards achieving our potential.

"We are in the process of appointing a sporting director who will have a direct input into the recruitment of a new permanent head coach."

Hodgson the oldest active coach in PL and EFL

Hodgson becomes the oldest head coach in the Championship - 20 years older than the closest to him in age, Sheffield United's Chris Wilder and Wrexham's Phil Parkinson.

The former Palace manager is also 15 years older than the previous oldest across the whole EFL, Swindon Town's Ian Holloway and Bristol Rover's Steve Evans.

Everton's David Moyes, the oldest Premier League manager, is 16 years younger than Hodgson.

Analysis: The ultimate unfinished business? Not quite...

Sky Sports' Ron Walker:

"16,038 days since he departed Bristol City in April 1982, his first managerial appointment in his native England, Roy Hodgson is back at Ashton Gate.

"It may be a stretch to call this unfinished business. But there is a level of sentimentality which has dragged the 78-year-old back into management for a seven-game stint, more than two years on from what had appeared to be his swansong role at Crystal Palace.

"Given he only managed City for four months all those years ago and lost 13 of his 22 games in charge, Hodgson's feeling likely owes more to a longing for one last roll of the dice than rose-tinted memories of his first spell at the club.

"From Bristol City's perspective, there is no sentimentality involved whatsoever.

"Previous incumbent Gerhard Struber was sacked despite remaining largely popular with the club's support. They placed the responsibility for a run of one win in nine at the feet of a board who had opted to sell top scorer Anis Mehmeti and key centre-back Zak Vyner in January after entering the last six months of their contracts.

"This was a body blow to their season on the pitch and in the dressing room, with the Robins one point and one place outside the top six at the time. Struber has since made his feelings known to that point publicly, but the Austrian must take some responsibility for their current slide too.

"A 5-1 thumping in Stoke at the start of November provided a hint of what was to come and by the time of the two players' departure, City had taken just 21 points from their previous 17 games.

"The club felt increasingly listless, with little hope of a required summer rebuild going ahead. Something had to change.

"Fans hoped that would come from the top, whether through Steve Lansdown's long-anticipated sale of the club finally going ahead, or the introduction of a more ambitious transfer policy to inspire them back to the play-off push they mounted last season.

"The board felt change was required from a different angle, with CEO Charlie Boss showing an early ruthless streak barely a month after his own appointment.

"More widely, Struber becomes the latest big personality to depart Ashton Gate after speaking out against the board - but the appointment of the oldest manager in the Football League, even on a temporary basis, does little to give the impression that a coherent alternative plan does exist."

Football.title: Szmodics out of hospital and 'on the mend' after collision
Football.content:
Republic of Ireland forward Sammie Szmodics has been released from hospital in Prague after being knocked unconscious during Thursday's World Cup play-off defeat by the Czech Republic.

The 24-year-old, who is currently on loan at Derby from Ipswich, was hurt in a collision with defender Stepan Chaloupek just two minutes after his introduction as an extra-time substitute at the Fortuna Arena, sparking concerned medics to rush to his aid.

Szmodics was carried from the field on a stretcher and taken straight to hospital, where Ireland head coach Heimir Hallgrimsson later revealed he would spend the night under observation.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Danyal Khan looks back on the heartbreaking result for the Republic of Ireland who lost their World Cup qualifying play-off against Czech Republic on penalties

A Football Association of Ireland spokesperson said on Friday morning: "Sammie Szmodics was transferred to hospital yesterday having sustained a head injury during extra time of the play-off against Czechia.

"Following further tests and a period of observation, he has been safely discharged from hospital and will continue his recovery under the care of the Republic of Ireland and Derby County medical teams."

Szmodics, who Hallgrimsson confirmed would have taken a penalty in the shoot-out which Ireland ultimately lost 4-3, took to social media to express his thanks to those who had helped him.

He posted on X: "Gutted the way it ended. Fans and boys immense all evening!! Appreciate everyone's messages. And thank you to the medical staff who acted so quickly to help me. On the mend."

Collins: Ireland must build on World Cup near miss

Ireland captain Nathan Collins is confident his side have laid the foundations to qualify for future tournaments having seen hopes of reaching this summer's World Cup finals come to an end.

Collins and his team-mates were agonisingly close to the play-off final when they led the Czech Republic 2-1 in Prague on Thursday evening with just four minutes of normal time remaining.

Image: A dejected Nathan Collins and head coach Heimir Hallgrimsson

However, Czech skipper Ladislav Krejci's late equaliser took the tie to extra-time and ultimately to penalties, where the hosts prevailed 4-3 to extend Ireland's wait for a first return to the big stage since 2002.

Asked about their progression to the play-offs, which was secured by back-to-back wins over Armenia, Portugal and Hungary, Brentford defender Collins said: "I'm not seeing many positives right now because we're in the gutter.

"But what we've created, the environment created with the fans and country, has been special. That's something we need to keep growing, to get even better at.

"That can help us get better on the pitch. We can push that and get fans to more tournaments."

Ireland had hoped to return to the Aviva Stadium on Tuesday for a showdown with either Denmark or North Macedonia for a place in the finals, but the Czechs will now host the Danes, who were 4-0 winners in Copenhagen.

Instead, Heimir Hallgrimsson's side face a friendly against the Macedonians.

Collins said: "The motivation has to be the same. We've to win to keep improving as a team and country. Any game we play at home is a big game."

Football.title: Tribal Chief fancied to strike gold in Lincoln
Football.content:
The flat is back! Kate Tracey is joined by Declan Rix and Sam Boswell to discuss the best betting angles for Doncaster's curtain-raiser.

The panel paid particular attention to the 3.32pm William Hill Lincoln, which sees 22 runners go in search of the £77,310 top prize on the Town Moor.

La Botte looks set to go off favourite for the Harry Eustace team, whilst William Haggas will be looking to notch a sixth win in this race with Eternal Force.

Declan Rix...

"We spoke to Harry Eustace on the website about La Botte in this and his comments were bolshy. I get the feeling this is the last time we see this 'fella' in a handicap, and this could be his day. His form stacks up and if you look at his prep run at Wolverhampton, watch how wide he swings round the bend and loses four or five lengths.

"His Royal Ascot and Newmarket form has been franked since and can completely respect him at the head of the market.

"One at a price I like is Urban Lion, as he is one of the prominent racers that goes well fresh. He could take some pegging back at a price for a yard that landed this with Johan a few years ago."

Kate Tracey…

"I am with two runners in the big field handicap, which are Tribal Chief and Great Acclaim. Both ran in the Balmoral handicap last year, in which a fair few of these runners lined up. Horses that get beat in the Balmoral especially finishing ninth to eleventh normally go close in this race - including Godwinson and Johan.

"So Tribal Chief looks to bounce back after a little disappointing last year but at 14-1 I think he could be primed for this for a trainer that has done well in the past in this race."

Sam Boswell…

"I like Greek Order in this at a price for Michael Bell and Jason Hart. He's an ex-Juddmonte performer and has been out to America, but ran creditably on yard debut when behind My Cloud. I think better ground will help his chances and it is a bit of a fact-finding mission - down in his mark I think he will go well.

"The yard are flying at the minute going at a 71% strike rate and there is no trainer in better form at the minute than Michael Bell. He has fitted Greek Order with the visor for the first time and I think he could be well-handicapped on old form."

Watch Weekend Winners in full on the At The Races YouTube channel...

Football.title: England v Uruguay buildup, World Cup playoffs reaction and more: football news – live
Football.content:
9m ago 09.49 EDT “It was the most important goal of my career,” said Sandro Tonali after his deadlock-breaker for Italy against Northern Ireland last night. Tonali, 25, is yet to play at a World Cup for Italy – they face Bosnia and Herzegovina next Tuesday for a place in the finals – and he was only little when they won the trophy in 2006. “I was six but I remember it all very clearly,” he said last year. “It’s probably the only thing I remember from when I was a kid.” Tonali’s national team boss, Gennaro Gattuso, was a childhood hero – he used to drink from a breakfast mug with Gattuso’s face on it. “That was my routine for many years. Then one day the cup broke and I begged my mother to glue it back together,” Tonali said. “When it was glued back together I had to leave it on a shelf. One day I’ll bring it to him and have him sign it.” AP View image in fullscreen Italy’s man of the hour. Photograph: Alberto Pizzoli/AFP/Getty Images Share

20m ago 09.38 EDT Thomas Tuchel will not field 11 of the 35 players in his bumper England squad for tonight’s friendly – they will come in for the second friendly against Japan on Tuesday, when as many as eight or nine other players will be released back to their clubs. Those 11 are: D Henderson, Burn, Guéhi, Konsa, O’Reilly, Anderson, Rice, Rogers, Gordon, Kane and Saka. That leaves a possible starting lineup tonight of: Pickford; Livramento, Stones, Maguire, Hall; Wharton, Mainoo; Madueke, Eze, Rashford; Solanke. Jude Bellingham will not feature tonight but has been training with the squad at Wembley. England hopefuls’ battle among themselves the key point of intrigue against Uruguay Read more Share

44m ago 09.14 EDT Summary Bristol City have appointed Roy Hodgson as interim manager until the end of the season after the sacking of Gerhard Struber

Everton will offer David Moyes a new contract this summer in recognition of his transformation of the club’s fortunes

David Bellamy says Wales ‘allowed chaos to creep in’ during the closing stages of their playoff defeat to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties

Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland’s hopes of qualifying for the World Cup are over after playoff exits

Bolivia and Jamaica have progressed to the final round of intercontinental playoffs in Mexico

Argentina striker Joaquín Panichelli has reportedly suffered a serious knee injury while away on international duty

Australia have beaten Cameroon 1-0 in a Fifa Series friendly in Sydney Share

1h ago 09.00 EDT Let’s check the mailbag … “Wales fan here. In one sense, I’m not too bothered about missing out on this tarnished World Cup in America. But I’m sad for the players. It would have been a great experience for the youngsters like Lawlor (what a talent he is, by the way), and a chance for the likes of Wilson, James and Brooks to show their skills at their peak. Of course, we would have had to beat Italy beforehand in any case, but I truly think we could have done it” – AJ Dylan Lawlor, at 20, looked like he’d been playing international football for years, didn’t he? Edin Dzeko, at 40, could’ve been his dad. Lawlor kept him quiet, in open play at least … Bewildered Bellamy searches for answers after Wales World Cup heartbreak Read more Share Updated at 09.14 EDT

1h ago 08.55 EDT The Strasbourg striker Joaquín Panichelli, the leading scorer in Ligue 1 this season, has injured his right knee while training ahead of Argentina’s friendly with Mauritania, the national team have said. Local media reports said Panichelli has a suspected cruciate ligament tear, putting him in doubt for Argentina’s World Cup title defence in the summer. “Joaquín Panichelli suffered a trauma to his right knee today and will be evaluated tomorrow with additional tests,” the Argentina team posted on X ahead of today’s friendly in Buenos Aires. The 23-year-old, who made his debut for Argentina in November, had previously suffered a cruciate ligament tear in July 2023, which sidelined him for more than six months. Panichelli is leading Ligue 1’s scoring charts with 16 goals this season. He has scored seven goals in his last nine appearances for Strasbourg in all competitions, helping them reach the quarter-finals of the Conference League. Reuters Share

1h ago 08.46 EDT Canada take on Iceland in a friendly in Toronto later. I went to Norwich last week to chat to Ali Ahmed, who says the World Cup co-hosts have the belief they can take on anyone this summer. Their group games – against Italy/Bosnia-Herzegovina, Qatar and Switzerland – are being held in Toronto (where Ahmed grew up) and Vancouver (where he broke into professional football). double quotation mark I want to win our group. And then from there, into the knockout rounds. It’s important to have that belief that we can play with anybody. Why not? It’s going to be a perfect setup for me, playing in Toronto and Vancouver – I couldn’t ask for anything better. View image in fullscreen Ali Ahmed (left) has made a splash in the Championship. Photograph: Stephen Pond/Getty Images Ahmed also discusses adapting to the Championship, playing during Ramadan and his teenage tour of Europe trialling for various, often lowly, youth teams. Canada’s Ali Ahmed on home World Cup dream: ‘I want to win our group’ Read more Share

1h ago 08.34 EDT Exclusive: Everton to offer Moyes new contract Matt Hughes Everton are planning to offer David Moyes a new contract this summer in recognition of his achievement in transforming the club from relegation candidates to challengers for European qualification. Moyes signed a two-and-a-half-year deal when succeeding Sean Dyche last January, with Everton one point above the relegation zone. They are currently eighth, only three points outside the Champions League qualification places. Moyes was initially seen as a short-term appointment by Everton’s owners, the Friedkin Group, who had only completed their £400m takeover the previous month. The Texan-based investors, led by chairman Dan Friedkin, are now convinced Moyes is the right man to take Everton forward in the longer term and want the 62-year-old to extend his contract. While formal discussions will not take place until the end of the season, there is confidence at Everton that Moyes is ready to extend his second spell at the club. View image in fullscreen More Moyes? Photograph: Robbie Jay Barratt/AMA/Getty Images Share

1h ago 08.29 EDT Nicolás Tagliafico is preparing to play in his third World Cup for Argentina, who are defending their title in the US this summer. The Lyon left-back has been speaking to AFP ahead of Argentina’s friendly against Mauritania tonight – their Finalissima against Spain in Qatar was cancelled because of war in the Middle East. double quotation mark Only Brazil and Italy have won two consecutive World Cups, but that was a long time ago. It’s a dream, like our dream of winning one, but we have to focus on the day-to-day, one match at a time, without getting carried away, because in the end, it’s the daily work that will pay off. Argentina have got Algeria, Austria and Jordan in their group in June, basing themselves in Kansas City where their opener (and possibly their quarter-final) is being played. Share
